questions.q: In the Ramayana, of which kind of being is Kumbhakarna a member?
questions.explanation: Kumbhakarna is a colossal rakshasa, the demon brother of Ravana, in the Ramayana.

questions.q: In the Ramayana, who is the elder brother of Kumbhakarna and king of Lanka?
questions.explanation: Ravana, the ten-headed king of Lanka, is the elder brother of Kumbhakarna.

questions.q: For what unusual habit is Kumbhakarna best known in the Ramayana?
questions.explanation: Kumbhakarna was famed for sleeping for six months at a stretch.

questions.q: Which ancient epic tells the story of Kumbhakarna?
questions.explanation: Kumbhakarna's story is told in Valmiki's Ramayana.

questions.q: In the Ramayana, who finally slays Kumbhakarna on the battlefield?
questions.explanation: Rama slays Kumbhakarna during the great war in the Ramayana.

questions.q: In the Ramayana, on which island kingdom does Kumbhakarna live?
questions.explanation: Kumbhakarna dwells in Lanka, the kingdom ruled by his brother Ravana.

questions.q: Which physical trait is Kumbhakarna most famous for in the Ramayana?
questions.explanation: Kumbhakarna is described as a giant of colossal size in the Ramayana.

questions.q: In the Ramayana, on which side of the great war does Kumbhakarna fight?
questions.explanation: Kumbhakarna fights loyally for his brother Ravana's army in the Ramayana.

questions.q: In the Ramayana, who is the wise, virtuous younger brother of Kumbhakarna who joins Rama?
questions.explanation: Vibhishana, brother of Kumbhakarna and Ravana, defects to Rama's side.

questions.q: In the Ramayana, what did Kumbhakarna advise his brother Ravana to do regarding Sita?
options: To send her to the devas
questions.explanation: Kumbhakarna warned Ravana that abducting Sita was wrong and counselled returning her.

questions.q: According to the Ramayana, which sage is the father of Kumbhakarna?
questions.explanation: The sage Vishrava is the father of Kumbhakarna, Ravana and Vibhishana.

questions.q: In the Ramayana, who is the mother of Kumbhakarna?
questions.explanation: Kaikesi, the rakshasa consort of Vishrava, is the mother of Kumbhakarna.

questions.q: In the Ramayana, from which deity did Kumbhakarna seek a boon through severe penance?
questions.explanation: Kumbhakarna performed austerities to win a boon from Brahma, the creator.

questions.q: According to the Ramayana, which goddess caused Kumbhakarna's boon request to come out garbled?
questions.explanation: The goddess Saraswati sat upon his tongue, so his request for a throne emerged as a wish for sleep.

questions.q: In the Ramayana, what did Kumbhakarna intend to ask Brahma for before the boon was confounded?
questions.explanation: He meant to ask for Indrasana, the seat of Indra, but instead asked for nidrasana, the bed of sleep.

questions.q: In the Ramayana, roughly how long was Kumbhakarna fated to sleep at a stretch?
questions.explanation: Owing to the garbled boon, Kumbhakarna slept for six months at a time.

questions.q: In the Ramayana, what means did Ravana's people use to wake the sleeping Kumbhakarna for the war?
options: A great clamour with elephants, drums and food
questions.explanation: They roused him with enormous noise, trampling elephants, and mountains of food and drink.

questions.q: In the Ramayana, what is Kumbhakarna chiefly described as devouring once roused for battle?
questions.explanation: Once awakened and enraged, Kumbhakarna devoured warriors on the battlefield.

questions.q: In the Ramayana, which monkey leader does Kumbhakarna seize and carry off during the battle?
questions.explanation: Kumbhakarna seizes the vanara king Sugriva, who later escapes by biting off his ears and nose.

questions.q: In the Ramayana, what weapon is most often associated with the giant Kumbhakarna in battle?
questions.explanation: Kumbhakarna is depicted wielding a massive mace or club as he storms the battlefield.

questions.q: According to the Ramayana, what feeling did Kumbhakarna express about Ravana's deeds even as he went to fight?
options: Disapproval, yet loyalty to his brother
questions.explanation: Kumbhakarna disapproved of Ravana's wrongdoing but chose to fight loyally for his brother.

questions.q: In the Ramayana, by what means does Rama ultimately bring down the giant Kumbhakarna?
options: By drowning him in the sea
By severing his limbs and head with arrows
By trapping him with a noose
questions.explanation: Rama cuts off Kumbhakarna's arms and finally his head with his arrows in the Ramayana.

questions.q: According to the Ramayana, who is the grandfather of Kumbhakarna through the line of his father Vishrava?
questions.explanation: Vishrava's father was the sage Pulastya, making him the grandfather of Kumbhakarna.

questions.q: In the Ramayana, which sister of Kumbhakarna is mutilated by Lakshmana, sparking the conflict?
questions.explanation: Shurpanakha, the sister of Kumbhakarna, is disfigured by Lakshmana, igniting the chain of events.

questions.q: According to the Ramayana, who is the son of Kumbhakarna who also fights and falls in the war?
questions.explanation: Kumbha and Nikumbha, sons of Kumbhakarna, fight in the war and are slain by the vanaras.

questions.q: According to the Bhagavata Purana tradition, Kumbhakarna and Ravana are said to be a heavenly incarnation of which figures cursed to be born as demons?
options: Jaya and Vijaya, gatekeepers of Vishnu
questions.explanation: Puranic tradition identifies Ravana and Kumbhakarna as the cursed gatekeepers Jaya and Vijaya in one of their demon births.

questions.q: In the Ramayana, what counsel did Kumbhakarna give Ravana about the war once he understood the situation?
options: To make peace by returning Sita, but to fight bravely if war was chosen
questions.explanation: Kumbhakarna chided Ravana's folly and urged returning Sita, yet vowed to fight valiantly if war was inevitable.

questions.q: According to the Ramayana, which celestial weapon does Rama invoke to finally behead Kumbhakarna?
questions.explanation: Rama looses the Indrastra, the weapon of Indra, to sever the head of the giant Kumbhakarna.

questions.q: In the Ramayana, what does Kumbhakarna do to Hanuman during their clash on the battlefield?
options: Wounds him with a thrown spear
questions.explanation: Kumbhakarna strikes and wounds Hanuman with his spear during their fierce encounter in the war.

questions.q: According to the Ramayana, into what does the falling body of the slain Kumbhakarna crash as he dies?
questions.explanation: The colossal body of Kumbhakarna topples into the sea by Lanka, churning its waters as he falls.
